seoTitle: Change Your Sign-In Email Address in Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Update the email tied to your Microsoft Teams account, whether you use a personal profile alias or an enterprise-managed work account.
intro: Because Teams identity is tied directly to your broader Microsoft account, changing your email address means following a different path depending on whether you're on a personal profile or an enterprise-managed directory. This guide covers both routes so you can update your sign-in details without losing your chat history.

steps.heading: Open your Microsoft account settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in Teams and select My Microsoft Account to launch the browser-based account console.

steps.heading: Add a new alias
steps.body: Under Your Info, use the account management panel to add a new email address as a secondary alias on your personal account.

steps.heading: Verify the new address
steps.body: Check your new inbox for the verification code sent by Microsoft and enter it to confirm ownership.

steps.heading: Make it your primary sign-in
steps.body: Once verified, click Make Primary so the new email becomes your default login credential.

steps.heading: Update enterprise accounts through the admin center
steps.body: For a work account, a system administrator needs to update the User Principal Name for your profile inside the Microsoft 365 Admin Center's Active Users section.

faq.question: Will I lose my chat history if I change my email?
faq.answer: No, adding a new alias and making it primary does not delete your existing chat history in Teams.

faq.question: Can I change my work email myself without an administrator?
faq.answer: No, enterprise account email changes typically require a system administrator to update your User Principal Name in the admin center.

faq.question: Do I need to log out after changing my email?
faq.answer: Yes, a full application logout and cache clear helps force Teams to recognize your updated sign-in profile.

faq.question: What's the difference between adding an alias and changing my primary email?
faq.answer: Adding an alias creates a secondary sign-in option, while making it primary sets that address as your default login credential.
